summary: The conjecture that every tournament of order $n$ contains at least $\lceil n(n-1)/6 - n/3 \rceil$ arc-disjoint transitive triples ($TT_3$) remains open. The best lower bound, $f(n) > \frac{41}{300}n^2(1+o(1))$, is due to Kabiya and Yuster (2008) and falls short of the conjectured value of $\approx n^2/6$. No post-2013 paper improving this bound or resolving the conjecture was found.
search_queries: transitive triple packing tournament arc-disjoint TT3 conjecture Yuster
arc-disjoint transitive subtournaments order 3 packing minimum tournament arXiv
Kabiya Yuster packing transitive triples tournament f(n) lower bound 2013 2014 2015 2016 2017 2018 2019 2020 2021 2022 2023 2024 2025
"transitive tournament tilings" "oriented graphs" "minimum total degree" arXiv
notes: arXiv:2306.02364 (tournament survey) and SIAM 10.1137/19M1269257 (transitive tournament tilings under degree conditions) address related but distinct problems. The SIAM paper concerns vertex-disjoint tilings with minimum degree conditions (Hajnal–Szemerédi type), not minimum arc-disjoint TT_3 packing. The NSF PDF and Kabiya–Yuster PDF were binary and unreadable by WebFetch. No verifiable post-2013 paper improving on the 41/300 lower bound or resolving the conjecture was found across 5 search queries.
